mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in 70% of instances where the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with declining industrial production indicates inventory depletion and supply bottlenecks that force price increases and compress profit margins, driving negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive forward returns in over 65% of triggered cases or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation during periods of near-zero policy rates forces markets to price in imminent monetary tightening, which compresses valuation multiples and produces negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally by more than 2% over 20 days following CPI spikes when policy rates remain below 0.25%, invalidating the tightening expectation channel.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect strong household balance sheets and construction sector activity, which immediately boost short-term market sentiment and generate positive 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: The housing starts signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over consecutive 1-year windows and fails to predict 5-day return direction at better than 50% accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the index of industrial production below 0.3% signals weakening manufacturing output that reduces corporate revenue growth expectations and triggers negative equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns exceed 1% in 60% of months where industrial production contracts by more than 0.3%, negating the revenue growth channel.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and real GDP confirms broad-based economic expansion that supports corporate earnings revisions and drives positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined payroll and GDP growth signal fails to produce positive 60-day returns in at least 55% of observations or shows no incremental predictive power over GDP alone.

mechanism_narrative: Falling unemployment alongside stable policy rates indicates an overheating labor market that compels central banks to normalize policy, creating valuation headwinds and negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Markets sustain positive 20-day returns in over 70% of periods where unemployment falls below 4% while rates remain near zero, disproving the overheating channel.
